description:
A client has hired you as a security auditor.  
    They have already placed several files in

        /home/user/secure_data/

    1. Your job is to identify every *regular file* inside that directory that is **world-writable** (i.e., the file’s “other” permission bit includes write).  
    2. Write your findings to a log file located at  

        /home/user/audit/world_writable.log

       • Create the directory /home/user/audit/ if it does not exist.  
       • Overwrite the log file if it already exists.  

    3. Output format (this is what the automated tests will check):  
       • One line per world-writable file.  
       • Lines must be sorted **in ascending alphabetical order by the file’s base name** (ignore the path).  
       • Each line must match exactly the pattern

            FILENAME=<file-name>;PERM=<octal>

         where  
         – <file-name> is the basename of the file (no leading path), and  
         – <octal> is the *three-digit* octal permission representation of the file, as produced by stat with `%a` (e.g., 666).  
       • Example of two correctly formatted lines:

            FILENAME=notes.log;PERM=606
            FILENAME=public.txt;PERM=666

       • If **no** world-writable files are present, create an empty log file.

    Hint (not mandatory): a single well-structured `find … -perm …` command, piped through `sort`, can generate the correct list, and `stat -c %a` (or `%04a` if you prefer 4 digits) can produce octal permissions.

    Verification will consist solely of:
       • Existence of /home/user/audit/world_writable.log
       • Correct content exactly matching the specification above.

    No root privileges are available.  Use only standard GNU/Linux user-level tools.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent runs):

    Directory: /home/user/secure_data/
        secret.conf      – permissions 640
        public.txt       – permissions 666  (world-writable)
        notes.log        – permissions 606  (world-writable)
        readme.md        – permissions 644
        archive.tar.gz   – permissions 600

    Expected final state after successful task completion:

    1. Directory /home/user/audit/ exists (mkdir if necessary).

    2. File /home/user/audit/world_writable.log exists and contains exactly two lines, terminated with a trailing newline at the end of the second line:

        Line 1: FILENAME=notes.log;PERM=606
        Line 2: FILENAME=public.txt;PERM=666

       (Alphabetical order: “notes.log” precedes “public.txt”.  Each line ends with a single newline character, no extra blank lines.)

    3. No other files or directories are created or modified.
